title: Application Layer
description: Covers sockets, HTTP, and web caching.

questions.prompt: What is the name for the API that the application layer uses to send and receive messages via the transport layer?
Which of the following are TRUE about sockets?
options: A socket is bound to a local IP address and port number
Processes send and receive messages through sockets
The application chooses between TCP and UDP when creating a socket
Each host can only have one open socket at a time
Lower layers are responsible for actually transporting the message to the receiver's socket

questions.prompt: Which version of HTTP reuses a single TCP connection for multiple objects (persistent connections)?
What is the name for an intermediate server that stores frequently-requested web content and serves it without involving the origin server?
Which of the following are benefits of web caching?
options: Reduces traffic on the ISP's external links
Guarantees every response is up to date

questions.prompt: Match each application-layer protocol to its typical purpose.
right: Dynamically assigns IP addresses to hosts on a network
Serves web pages and resources to clients
Resolves domain names to IP addresses

questions.prompt: Which of the following are TRUE about HTTP v1.0 (non-persistent) vs HTTP v1.1 (persistent)?
options: v1.0 opens a new TCP connection for each object
v1.1 can send multiple objects over a single TCP connection
v1.1 total time for n objects is roughly (n+1) x RTT plus download time, whereas v1.0 is roughly 2n x RTT plus download time (simplified)
v1.1 is worse for server resource usage than v1.0
v1.0 has lower per-request overhead than v1.1
